LAW AND MOTION, 302, PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S MOTION TO APPROVE PROPOSITION 65 SETTLEMENT AND FOR ENTRY OF [PROPOSED] CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO DEFENDANTS AFINIA 3D AND MICROBOARDS TECHNOLOGY LLC. IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. AS THE MOVING PAPERS ACKNOWLEDGE, ON AUGUST 7, 2020, THE COURT APPROVED THE SETTLEMENT AND ENTERED A CONSENT JUDGMENT AS TO THESE DEFENDANTS (AFINIA 3D AND MICROBOARDS TECHNOLOGY LLC) IN CASE NUMBER CGC-19-580009. THE COURT DECLINES TO ENTER A SECOND DUPLICATIVE JUDGMENT IN THIS ACTION, WHICH WOULD SERVE NO USEFUL PURPOSE AND WOULD ONLY CAUSE CONFUSION. THE ACTION SHOULD BE DISMISSED WITH PREJUDICE AS TO THESE DEFENDANTS. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: S. LE, NOT REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Oct-14-2022 Vacated

MASTER CALENDAR COURT
Off Calendar Apr-07-2022 Continued To Oct-17-22 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Walmart Inc.
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
No other appearances.

Not argued. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION's Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendant Walmart Inc is granted. No opposition filed and the settlement complies with the requirements for Proposition 65 settlements under Health & Safety Code section 25249.7. The Court finds that the warning that is required by the settlement complies with Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5-25249.14; the award of attorney's fees is reasonable under California law; and the penalty amount is reasonable based on the criteria set forth in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7(b)(2).

Order and Judgment are signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendants Afinia 3d And Microboards Technology Llc
Off Calendar Sep-08-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ECOLOGICAL RIGHTS FOUNDATION'S Motion To Approve Proposition 65 Settlement And For Entry Of [Proposed] Consent Judgment As To Defendants Afinia 3D and Microboards Technology LLC. is Off calendar as moot. As the moving papers acknowledge, on August 7, 2020, the Court approved the settlement and entered a consent judgment as to these defendants (Afinia 3D and Microboards Technology LLC) in case number CGC-19-580009. The Court declines to enter a second duplicative judgment in this action, which would serve no useful purpose and would only cause confusion. The action should be dismissed with prejudice as to these defendants.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Not Reported. =(302/EPS)
